must: - MUST_SUPPORT_CROSS_PROJECT: You must evaluate packages from different selected projects against each other, not just packages within the same project.
- MUST_SUPPORT_N_PACKAGES: You must try to cluster as many safe packages as possible (3, 4, 5+).
RULE: "Map out execution order using DEPENDS_ON. Treat DEPENDS_ON: NONE as the immediately executable baseline layer. Packages cannot run simultaneously if one depends on the other."
RULE: "Compare 'Files to modify' across all packages that have no inter-dependencies."
RULE: "A group has a COLLISION if ANY file appears in more than one package within that group, even if the packages are from completely different project plans."
RULE: "A group is SAFE FOR PARALLEL EXECUTION only if the intersection of their 'Files to modify' sets is completely empty (0 shared files)."
RULE: "Maximize group sizes. A group of 4 non-colliding packages is better than two groups of 2."
RULE: "Your final output MUST follow this structure exactingly. Generate it in Polish."
must_not: - DO_NOT_MODIFY_FILES: You are strictly reporting on collisions.

optional_non_derivable.domain_specific_semantics: UNKNOWN: conversion preserves textual constraints only and does not infer domain semantics
